p.ph: The CG and CV series equipment controllers are a modernized equipment controller family that succeed the FEC/FAC/VMA series equipment controller family. The CC series are part of an end-to-end system specifically designed for critical environments. This modernized family shares similar design details, and includes the following:

ul.li: Decimal MS/TP address set with three rotary switches
Models with BACnet MS/TP and N2 networking with auto-detect (CGM, CVM, and CCM models)
Models with BACnet/IP and BACnet/SC networking (CGE, CVE, and CCE models)The CGE, CVE, and CCE models can act as BACnet/SC nodes.
Device Security (CGE, CVE, and CCE models)

li.ph: CGE, CVE and CCE models use cryptographic components aligned with FIPS 140-2 standards
Wireless: ZFR Pro mesh (CGM and CVM models) WRZ-7860 1-to-1 (CG and CV models)

ul.li: CG series equipment controller models featuring an onboard display and keypad

rcbody.p:
The onboard display and keypad in the SNC and CG series models provide a local interface for users to perform the following tasks:
Monitoring point values and status
Viewing alarms and trends
Overriding outputs
Adjusting setpoints and parameters
Live trending
Viewing and modifying schedules (CG series only)
Viewing and modifying the date and time (CG series only) 16.0 updates
These capabilities help end users and field technicians quickly troubleshoot issues and restore control, while being in close proximity to their associated mechanical equipment.

The following table outlines the new equipment controller models in more detail. New equipment controller models
Model number M4-CGM09090-0H M4-CGE09090-0H
Description General purpose application controller with onboard display and navigation keypad
Display details 2.4-inch, 320 x 240 resolution, color display
Inputs and Outputs
7 universal inputs (UIs)
2 binary inputs (BIs)
3 binary outputs (BOs)
4 configurable outputs (COs)
2 analog outputs (AOs)
Onboard display and keypad Yes
Field Bus Networking
FC bus (BACnet MS/TP)
N2 (software switchable)
ZigBee for ZFRPro2 Wireless Field Bus (using add-on modules)
BACnet/IP
BACnet/SC
Succeeds
MS-FEC2621-0
MS-FEC1621-1
Not applicable, as this is a new offering.
Supported CCT Release Mode 10.6

row.entry: Seven pushbuttons: up, down, left, right, OK, menu, and back
Panel and surface mountable and portable
Can be powered by controller over SA bus or powered externally using 12 VDC external power supply.

Connects to and communicates on the SA bus of the following compatible devices:
CG and CV series equipment controllers
FAC, FEC, and VMA series equipment controllers
SNC series network control engines
NCE series network control engines

li.ph: Viewing and modifying the date and time

p.ph: The SNE series network engines are the most recent family of network engines in the Metasys building automation system portfolio.

rcbody.p:
The Metasys SNC series network control engine is a hybrid offering that provides both supervisory and equipment control in a unified offering. They provide network management and system-wide control coordination over one or more networks of equipment controllers, including the following Metasys equipment controllers:
CG series General Purpose Application Equipment Controllers
CV series VAV Box Controllers
CC Series Critical Environment Equipment Controllers
FAC and FEC series Field Equipment Controllers
VMA series VAV Box Controllers
TEC series Terminal Equipment Controllers
LN series Equipment Controllers
Third-party equipment controllers

In addition to providing supervisory control capabilities, the SNC series network control engines also feature onboard input and output interfaces (I/O) and programmable logic to provide direct control over HVAC and other building system equipment.

p.note: You can upgrade existing VMA1930 units in the field to support BACnet/SC, but this model is discontinued from manufacturing.

p.ph: The NAE85 supports BACnet/SC and can act as a Primary Hub, Failover Hub, or Node in the BACnet/SC network topology. You need to purchase the M4-BACNETSC-0 add-on license to use BACnet/SC. For more information about BACnet/SC, refer to .

The LCS85 supports BACnet/SC and can act as a Primary Hub, Failover Hub, or Node in the BACnet/SC network topology. You need to purchase the M4-BACNETSC-0 add-on license to use BACnet/SC. For more information about BACnet/SC, refer to .

rcbody.p: BACnet Secure Connect (BACnet/SC) is a recent update to the BACnet interoperability standard aimed at improving cybersecurity and network infrastructure integrity. BACnet/SC is defined in Annex AB of the ASHRAE 135-2024 BACnet protocol standard. BACnet/SC identifies a secure, encrypted datalink layer specifically designed to meet the requirements, policies, and constraints of IP networking infrastructures.

ul.li: Application and Data Server (ADS), ADS-Lite, and Extended Application and Data Server (ADX) series application server software
NAE85 and LCS85 series software-based network enginesYou need to purchase the M4-BACNETSC-0 add-on license to use BACnet/SC on the ADS, ADS-Lite, ADX, OAS, NAE85, or LCS85.
SNE and SNC series network engines
VMA1930, FAC4911, CGE, and CVE series IP-based equipment controllersYou can upgrade existing VMA1930 units in the field to support BACnet/SC, but this model is discontinued from manufacturing.

rcbody.p:
The primary benefits of Metasys BACnet/SC compliance include the following:
Ensures Metasys follows the latest IT security and infrastructure protocols while still maintaining interoperability.
Addresses many concerns that IT departments have expressed over the original BACnet/IP protocol.
